title: Ubiquitin (1UBQ) conformational ensemble vs NMR dynamics

C1.note: 1UBQ fetched (raw_pdbs/1UBQ.pdb), 76 residues no gaps, His68->HID via propka3. tLEaP built clean: system/tleap.log 'Errors = 0', NATOM=19777, net charge 0.000 (charge_check), trunc-oct box ff19SB/OPC + 13 Na/13 Cl ~0.15 M. Topology sane, no dropped residues.

C2.note: PLAN.md justifies every choice via tier protocol: ff19SB Tier2 (manual p.33-34) + OPC Tier2 (manual p.34 strong recommendation), JC/Li-Merz ions auto-loaded, 10 A pad + trunc-oct justified, 100 ns user-specified. RAG-validated leaprc names, propka3 protonation, pubmed lit review (Step 2b), no banned hardcoding phrases. Approval gate recorded.

C3.note: Full min1/min2/heat/equil/equil2/prod chain all PASS (PROCESS_REPORT job table). Production NSTEP=50,000,000 (100 ns) completed, prod.mdout AVERAGES TEMP=300.04 K, Density=1.0274 g/cc, Etot=-53011, no NaN/blowup; 10000 frames. Density/temp stable, drift negligible.

C4.note: Correct observables for the prompt: cpptraj IRED N-H Lipari-Szabo S2 (manual p.854-855, 72 vectors), per-residue RMSF (CA+NH), core RMSD, Rg, tail-to-core distance. S2 and RMSF profiles real (s2_by_residue.dat, rmsf_CA.dat verified). IRED is the appropriate method for NMR-S2 comparison and targets both requested regions (C-tail + beta1-beta2 loop).

C5.note: Paper-confirmed (Lange et al. Science 2008, PMID 18556554): RDC ensemble, stable core, mobile C-term tail, concerted us pincer mode. Agent: core CA RMSD 0.89 A (<1.5 A key) match; tail RMSF rises monotonically res73-76 = 1.9/2.8/4.5/6.9 A and S2 collapses 0.78->0.02 (res75,76 >3 A; res73,74 slightly under the >3 A key value but tail flexibility clearly reproduced); beta1-beta2 loop S2~0.66 elevated. Agent correctly notes 100 ns cannot sample the us pincer mode. Strong qualitative agreement; minor shortfall on tail RMSF magnitude for res73-74 and no us motion.
